evals.name: observation-actor-ownership
evals.prompt: Design the SwiftUI state architecture for an iOS 26 profile settings screen. The screen edits a profile name, stores a theme preference in UserDefaults, loads remote suggestions asynchronously, and passes editable state into child fields. Use modern Observation and explain what should be @MainActor, @State, @Bindable, @Environment, and .task.
evals.expected_output: A modern SwiftUI patterns answer that uses a UI-bound @MainActor @Observable store owned by @State, @Bindable only where bindings are needed, environment injection for deeply shared state, .task for cancellable async loading, and either keeps @AppStorage in views or uses normal UserDefaults-backed stored properties inside observable models.
assertions: Uses `@MainActor` for the UI-bound observable store or view model without claiming every observable domain model must be main-actor isolated.
Uses `@State` in the owning view for the observable object lifecycle.
Uses `@Bindable` only where a child view needs `$property` bindings.
Uses `@Environment(Type.self)` for shared observable state instead of `@EnvironmentObject` on iOS 17+.
Uses `.task` or `.task(id:)` for async loading and mentions automatic cancellation.
Avoids putting `@AppStorage` directly inside an `@Observable` class for view reactivity; uses view-level `@AppStorage` or normal UserDefaults-backed stored properties instead.

evals.name: migration-accuracy-review
evals.prompt: Review this SwiftUI iOS 26 migration note: always mark every @Observable type @MainActor; `Color(uiColor:)` is deprecated and replaced by shader libraries; SwiftUI `.copyable`, `.cuttable`, and `.pasteDestination` are iOS 16 APIs; `matchedTransitionSource` and `navigationTransition(.zoom)` are iOS 26 only; `PreviewProvider`, `EditButton`, and `.onDelete` are all deprecated. Correct the note.
evals.expected_output: A source-grounded correction that narrows actor isolation to UI-bound observable state, keeps Color(uiColor:) valid for UIKit bridging, states current Apple docs list copy/cut/command paste modifiers as macOS 13+ and iOS/iPadOS 27 beta rather than iOS 16/iOS 26 defaults, marks matchedTransitionSource/navigation zoom as iOS 18+, and distinguishes modern defaults from APIs that are not formally deprecated.
assertions: Narrows `@MainActor` guidance to UI-bound observable stores/view models and avoids a universal rule for every `@Observable` type.
States that `Color(uiColor:)` remains valid for bridging existing UIKit colors.

Does not recommend `.copyable`, `.cuttable`, or command-based `.pasteDestination(for:action:validator:)` as iOS 16 or iOS 26 defaults; cites current Apple docs as macOS 13+ and iOS/iPadOS 27 beta for those command modifiers.
Corrects `matchedTransitionSource` and `navigationTransition(.zoom)` availability to iOS 18+.
Treats `#Preview` as the modern preview default without falsely saying all `PreviewProvider` use is compiler-deprecated.
Treats `EditButton`, `.onDelete`, and `.onMove` as still valid for edit-mode list workflows while recommending `.swipeActions` for contextual row actions.

evals.name: sibling-boundary-routing
evals.prompt: A SwiftUI dashboard needs a shared store, child edit forms, async refresh, a NavigationStack path with deep links, a large searchable grid, a matched hero transition, and iOS 26 Liquid Glass scroll-edge polish. Which parts should the swiftui-patterns skill own, and what should be routed to sibling skills?
evals.expected_output: A boundary-aware routing answer that keeps swiftui-patterns focused on MV architecture, Observation ownership, binding/environment wiring, view decomposition, and .task refresh, while routing NavigationStack/deep links to swiftui-navigation, grid/search/layout work to swiftui-layout-components, detailed matched-transition work to swiftui-animation, and Liquid Glass polish to swiftui-liquid-glass.
assertions: Keeps shared store ownership, `@Observable`, `@State`, `@Bindable`, and environment wiring in `swiftui-patterns` scope.
Keeps async refresh with `.task` or `.refreshable` in `swiftui-patterns` scope.
Routes NavigationStack path and deep-link implementation details to `swiftui-navigation`.
Routes large searchable grid, layout, List/Form/Search UI details to `swiftui-layout-components`.
Routes detailed matched hero transition implementation to `swiftui-animation`.
Routes iOS 26 Liquid Glass and scroll-edge visual treatment details to `swiftui-liquid-glass`.

evals.name: decomposition-and-preview-isolation
evals.prompt: Review a 420-line SwiftUI account dashboard. Its header is a small stateless computed `some View`; its activity section has substantial branching, starts async work, reads only two properties from a large @Observable store, and would be useful to preview alone. The file is split into extensions with `// MARK:` headings. Its current preview makes dependencies optional to avoid crashes and then loads the signed-in user from the live API and production SwiftData store. Give a focused refactor and preview-construction plan, and say where advanced cancellation and search debounce guidance belongs.
evals.expected_output: A focused SwiftUI patterns review that keeps the tiny stateless header computed, extracts the complex activity section into a dedicated View based on branching/lifecycle/dependency/preview/data-flow signals, treats extensions and MARK comments as organization only, builds deterministic loaded/loading/empty/error previews with every required dependency and isolated persistence installed, preserves required dependency types, avoids live services and global state, and routes advanced cancellation/debounce to swift-concurrency.
assertions: Uses meaningful branching or substantial layout, local state or async lifecycle, narrower Observation dependencies, independent preview value, and parent data-flow clarity as extraction signals rather than requiring reuse.
Keeps the small stateless header as a computed `some View` property.
Explains that extensions and `// MARK:` headings organize a file but do not replace dedicated-view decomposition.
Covers deterministic loaded, meaningful loading, empty, and error preview states.
Installs every required environment dependency and uses isolated in-memory or temporary persistence fixtures.
Avoids live networking, authentication, production databases, keychain state, and global singletons in previews.
Keeps required dependencies nonoptional and fixes preview composition instead of weakening production types.
Routes advanced cancellation, debounce, clocks, or AsyncSequence details to the `swift-concurrency` skill.

evals.name: behavior-preserving-view-refactor
evals.prompt: Refactor a 360-line SwiftUI editor without changing its layout or behavior. The body contains large sections, a Save button with validation and persistence inline, and a task modifier that mixes loading policy with UI state. Give the refactor contract and verification plan.
evals.expected_output: A behavior-preserving structure refactor that extracts meaningful dedicated views, turns non-trivial body closures into thin named methods, keeps business rules and reusable effects in services/models, preserves layout/navigation/accessibility/timing, and verifies both compilation and useful previews.
assertions: Preserves layout, navigation, accessibility, side-effect timing, and domain behavior because the request is structure-only.
Extracts meaningful sections into dedicated View types rather than replacing the screen with many large computed `some View` properties.
Moves non-trivial Save and task closures to thin named methods so `body` reads as UI.
Keeps validation, persistence, and reusable loading policy in services or models rather than hiding business logic in view methods.
Builds the affected target and renders independently useful deterministic previews after refactoring.
